examples: The safety-first attitude of clubs in postponing matches, despite playable pitches, appears to be undermining the point of undersoil heating, but caution in the face of a cold snap is understandable.
The pitch has undersoil hearing and roads around the stadium have been gritted.
Settlers would stack brick upon brick of sod about an acre's worth until they had a home, then plant crops in the rich undersoil.
The forecast is for the freezing weather to continue into the weekend, making it almost impossible for clubs with no undersoil heating to make their pitches playable.
